skill.name: gws-gmail-send
skill.description: Send Gmail messages with recipients, subject, body, aliases, copies, drafts, HTML, and attachments through the gws CLI.
skill.summary: Send or draft Gmail messages from AI-assisted workflows using the gws CLI.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell backticks, path traversal, and weak cryptography are false positives in Markdown documentation and table syntax. The skill still has medium operational risk because it instructs an agent to run a Gmail write command that can send messages and attach local files, so user confirmation is required.

medium_findings.title: Gmail Write Command Requires User Confirmation
medium_findings.description: The skill documents a command that sends Gmail messages and explicitly warns that it is a write command. Sending email can disclose content to recipients or create irreversible external side effects if executed without user approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command purpose and caution text are explicit in the skill documentation. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the file tells the agent to confirm before execution.

medium_findings.title: Attachment Handling Can Expose Local Files
medium_findings.description: The skill supports attaching files to outgoing Gmail messages. This is legitimate functionality, but it can disclose local file contents if the agent attaches the wrong file or skips confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The attachment flag and examples are clearly documented. The risk depends on user-selected files and recipient confirmation, so it is medium rather than high.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Shell Execution Detections Are Markdown Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited content is Markdown inline formatting and fenced bash examples for using the gws CLI. No executable Ruby code, dynamic shell construction, or command injection path is present in the skill file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The only evidence is inside SKILL.md documentation, including a Markdown code fence and examples. There is no script file or runtime code in the scanned file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Path Traversal Detections Are Documentation Links
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyzer reported path traversal sequences at relative links to sibling skill documentation. These links reference documentation files and do not read arbitrary user-controlled paths or perform filesystem access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The relative paths appear in Markdown prerequisite and see-also references. No file read logic or user-controlled path construction is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Weak Cryptography Detections Are Not Cryptographic Use
dangerous_patterns.description: The analyzer reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the cited lines are front matter description and a Markdown table header. No hashing, encryption, or security decision uses a weak algorithm in the scanned file.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive Markdown, not cryptographic code. The scanned file is documentation only, so the static blocker pattern is not semantically supported.

content.user_title: Send Gmail Messages from AI Workflows
content.value_statement: Sending structured Gmail messages from an AI session can require careful command construction. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through the gws CLI send workflow with recipient, body, draft, HTML, alias, and attachment options.
actual_capabilities: Shows the required gws command for sending Gmail messages.
Documents recipient, subject, and body flags for plain text email.
Supports CC, BCC, send-as aliases, HTML bodies, drafts, and attachments.
Provides examples for single and multiple file attachments.
Warns that sending email is a write action that needs confirmation.
limitations: Requires the gws CLI to be installed and authenticated separately.
Does not manage Gmail authentication within this skill file.
Does not verify recipients, attachment contents, or message accuracy automatically.
Uses Gmail attachment limits and account send-as configuration.

use_cases.title: Send Follow-Up Notes
use_cases.description: Prepare and send concise Gmail follow-up messages after meetings or support conversations.

use_cases.title: Draft Reviewable Client Emails
use_cases.description: Create Gmail drafts with clear subject lines, recipients, and message bodies for manual review.

use_cases.title: Share Reports With Attachments
use_cases.description: Send reports or exports through Gmail with one or more explicitly selected attachments.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Gmail Send
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a simple plain text message.
prompt_templates.prompt: Send a Gmail message to [recipient] with subject [subject] and body [message]. Confirm the final command with me before sending.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Draft First
prompt_templates.scenario: Safer workflow when the user wants review before delivery.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Gmail draft to [recipient] about [topic]. Use [body details], and do not send it until I approve.

prompt_templates.title: Send With Copies and Alias
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow with recipients and a configured send-as address.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a Gmail message from [alias] to [primary recipients], CC [cc recipients], and BCC [bcc recipients]. Use subject [subject] and body [body].

prompt_templates.title: Send HTML With Attachments
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with formatting and files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare an HTML Gmail message to [recipients] with subject [subject], body [HTML fragment], and attachments [file paths]. List the recipients and files for confirmation before execution.

output_examples.input: Send Alice a short hello message.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a Gmail send action with Alice as the recipient, a clear subject, and a short plain text body, then asks for confirmation.

output_examples.input: Draft a report email with two attachments.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates a draft-focused plan that includes the recipients, subject, message body, and the two selected files for review.

output_examples.input: Send an HTML update from my team alias.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares an HTML email using the configured alias and confirms the formatted content before sending.

best_practices: Confirm recipients, subject, body, and attachments before sending.
Use draft mode for sensitive or external messages.
Use explicit file paths and inspect attachment names before execution.
anti_patterns: Do not send messages without user approval.
Do not attach files unless the user explicitly selected them.
Do not include secrets, tokens, or private data in email bodies.

faq.question: Does this skill send email directly?
faq.answer: It documents the gws CLI command for sending Gmail messages. The agent still needs access to the CLI and user confirmation.

faq.question: Can it save a Gmail draft instead of sending?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents a draft option so users can review messages before delivery.

faq.question: Can it send HTML email?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports HTML fragments when the HTML flag is used.

faq.question: Can it attach files?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports one or more attachments, subject to Gmail size limits.

faq.question: Does it configure Gmail authentication?
faq.answer: No. Authentication and global gws settings are handled by the shared Google Workspace skill documentation.

faq.question: Is it safe for automated email sending?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill itself warns that sending email is a write action and should be confirmed by the user.
